mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, mechanically lifting present valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid deterioration in labor demand that triggers recession fears, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the UNRATE threshold breach, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of real consumer credit and industrial production indicates a synchronized demand shock that erodes corporate earnings growth expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term (RSAFS decline × INDPRO decline) fails to explain more than 5% of the variance in 20-day forward returns, or the joint signal predicts negative returns when SPX actually rises in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a sustained rise in housing starts acts as a leading indicator of resilient aggregate demand, which overrides supply-side constraints and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns when CPIAUCSL exceeds 240, or the conditional alpha becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) in a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP over consecutive quarters reflects expanding economic activity that boosts corporate revenue growth, mechanically supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to correlate positively with 20-day forward returns, or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 50% across all available data points.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, the cost of capital becomes sufficiently restrictive to compress equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon as investors reprice risk.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS breaches 4.0%, or the signal produces false negatives in > 60% of high-rate observations over a 3-year test.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous acceleration of payroll employment and real consumer credit signals a wage-price spiral risk that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, ultimately depressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS and RSAFS acceleration fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns, or the interaction term's predictive power vanishes (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion where the unemployment rate remains below 4.0%, a sustained decline in industrial production signals capacity exhaustion and profit margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to generate negative 20-day forward returns when UNRATE is below 4.0%, or the conditional signal's directional accuracy drops below 45% over a rolling 2-year window.
